content.problemStatement:
You start with an integer X=0X = 0X=0. For NNN turns, you do **exactly one** of the following:


Either, you add 111 to XXX
Or, you add 333 to XXX
For example, for N=2N = 2N=2, you can choose to add 111 on the first turn, and 333 on the second turn, thus a final value of X=4X = 4X=4.

Given an integer MMM, print whether it is possible that the final value of XXX is MMM or not.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains 222 integers - NNN and MMM.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line YES\text{YES}YES if it possible to form MMM with exactly NNN turns, and NO\text{NO}NO otherwise.


It is allowed to print each character in either case, Yes\text{Yes}Yes, yes\text{yes}yes and yEs\text{yEs}yEs will all be accepted as positive responses.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1, 2, 3 : In one turn, you can add 111 or 333, thus obtaining either X=1X = 1X=1 or X=3X = 3X=3. Hence, M=1M = 1M=1 and M=3M = 3M=3 are achievable but not M=2M = 2M=2.
